content.plain_english: This week in Year 1 maths, your child is working on Mass and volume. The learning focuses on: compare, describe and solve practical problems for:; lengths and heights; and mass / weight. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children use practical measuring equipment — rulers, scales, measuring cylinders, and clocks — to explore measurement in real contexts. They record findings using diagrams and number lines before progressing to written calculations. Problem-solving tasks show how these skills are used every day.
content.home_activity: Collect kitchen items and estimate which is heavier before checking on a kitchen scale. Order them lightest to heaviest. For older children, find the combined mass of two items and work out the difference between them.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to compare, describe and solve practical problems for:?
Was it easy or tricky to lengths and heights? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The amount of space a 3-D shape takes up, measured in cubic units.
The maximum amount a container can hold.
A unit for measuring volume — a 1 cm × 1 cm × 1 cm cube.
A unit of capacity — 1 litre = 1000 millilitres.
A small unit of capacity — 1000 ml = 1 litre.

content.times_table_tip: Year 1 focuses on the 2, 5, and 10 times tables. Try counting in 2s together on the stairs, or in 10s while counting out coins. Even 2–3 minutes a day builds the familiarity that pays off in Year 2.
